title: Australian Clinical Labs Limited — Consumer harm
investigation_objective: The Federal Court ordered Australian Clinical Labs Limited (ACL) to pay $5.8 million in civil penalties — the first civil penalties ever ordered under the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) — in relation to a February 2022 data breach of its Medlab Pathology business, in which a threat actor exfiltrated approximately 86 gigabytes of data including the personal information of at least 223,000 individuals. The penalty comprised $4.2 million for failing to take reasonable steps to protect personal information (APP 11.1), $800,000 for failing to carry out a reasonable and expeditious assessment of whether an eligible data breach had occurred, and $800,000 for failing to properly notify the Australian Information Commissioner of the breach.
scope: First-ever civil penalty ordered under the Privacy Act 1988, totalling $5.8 million; Data breach exfiltrated personal information of at least 223,000 individuals; Penalty included separate contraventions for inadequate breach assessment and failure to properly notify the regulator
